private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
KP Labs is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company founded by Grzegorz Kasprowicz. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies spanning Series A and Series B funding rounds, alongside observation of six public Space & Aerospace comparables for sector context.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable valuation lens. The four private comparables—ClearSpace ($400M), Hubble Network ($400M), Aalyria ($1.3B), and Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B)—yield a median valuation of $850M, with a range spanning $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be applied because KP Labs' revenue is not disclosed; the median P/S multiple across six public Space & Aerospace names is 64.2x, but without target revenue this method yields no usable signal. Accordingly, analysis rests entirely on private peer benchmarking.

The triangulated valuation range is $722M to $977M, centered on $850M median. This range reflects the dispersion within the peer cohort and stage-relative positioning. Key uncertainties include KP Labs' actual revenue run-rate, capital deployment progress, and competitive standing relative to the four named peers—factors that would justify positioning toward either end of the $722M–$977M band. The single-method foundation also limits conviction; public revenue disclosure or additional Series A comparables would strengthen the analysis.
